Long, long before humanity mapped the world, the sky was ruled by a vain wind spirit named Zephira. She was woven from morning mist and starlight, and her voice was a melody that could coax winter into spring. Zephira loved nothing more than hearing herself sing, sweeping through valleys and canyons merely to listen to her own resonance.

Deep within the dark, cold earth lived Kaelen, the guardian of solid stone. He was silent, heavy, and deeply enamored with Zephira. Every dawn, he would wait for her song to brush against his craggy peaks. One day, unable to contain his longing, Kaelen struck his chest, creating a booming drumbeat in an attempt to harmonize with her.

Startled and offended by the coarse noise, Zephira cursed him. "If you must speak," she declared, her voice cold as a blizzard, "you shall only ever speak my words, and only after I have finished!"

Heartbroken, Kaelen’s form crumbled into the cavernous ravines. To this day, the mountain remains silent until spoken to. When you shout into a canyon, it is not just sound bouncing off rock. It is Kaelen, bound by an ancient curse, faithfully repeating the words of the cruel wind forever.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, the world knew only eternal summer. The Sun God, Solis, loved the Earth Goddess, Terra, so deeply that he never blinked, bathing her constantly in his golden, warm gaze. Flowers bloomed endlessly, and the fruit never dropped from the trees. 

But this perfection grew heavy. The rivers began to dry, and the creatures of the world grew exhausted from the relentless light. Terra, seeing her children suffer, pleaded with Solis to give them rest. Solis, stubborn and afraid that looking away meant he loved her less, refused. 

Desperate, Terra wove a magnificent cloak from the dying amber leaves and the cool twilight shadows. She threw the cloak over the sky, momentarily blinding Solis. Hurt, he retreated below the horizon, taking his warmth with him. The sudden shock turned the remaining leaves crimson and gold, and they fell to the ground in sudden grief. 

When Solis finally peeked over the horizon the next morning, he saw the breathtaking beauty of the changed world. Realizing his constant gaze had stifled Terra, he agreed to close his eyes for a season each year. Thus, Autumn was born—a beautiful, fleeting reminder that rest is just as necessary as light.

Gaia, however, preferred the cool, quiet dark of her deep caverns, overwhelmed by his brilliant, deafening radiance. 

Desperate to win her, Aether cast his brightest, loudest melody down upon the mountains, shattering the peaks with his golden heat. Heartbroken by the destruction of her quiet world, Gaia wove a thick, soft blanket from crushed mountain stones and river water. She exhaled this heavy breath upward, creating a dense, impenetrable, swirling white veil between the earth and the sky. 

Aether’s glowing songs struck the veil, but instead of simply burning the earth, his voice was scattered safely into a million soft, shimmering drops of light. Defeated but mesmerized, Aether learned to quiet his roar to a gentle whisper just to see the veil glow. 

This was the magical birth of the morning fog. To this day, the mist gently rises when the earth seeks quiet peace, forcing the once boastful sun to soften his harsh, blazing voice before he can ever touch the ground again.
